name: Assessment Stakes Consequential Use Boundaries Update
summary: Staged targeted review update for consequential and lower-stakes synthetic-response use boundaries, adding direct operational state summative evidence and bounded high-stakes, formative, and classroom augmentation evidence.

scope.research_question: What uses of synthetic student responses are acceptable across high-stakes, summative, formative, in-term, classroom, and other consequential automated-scoring contexts?

proposed_changes.summary: Stage official New Mexico MSSA technical report as direct operational summative evidence.
proposed_changes.rationale: The report documents LLM-generated synthetic responses used as limited training augmentation for operational automated Writing scoring.

proposed_changes.summary: Stage high-stakes spoken assessment off-topic detection paper as adjacent boundary evidence.
proposed_changes.rationale: The paper clarifies that synthetic or constructed response sets can support validity-protection detection without becoming automated-score validation.

proposed_changes.summary: Stage BEA 2025 formative communication-skills scoring paper.
proposed_changes.rationale: The paper adds lower-stakes professional-education evidence for synthetic training augmentation with real-response evaluation.

proposed_changes.summary: Stage NARST/arXiv classroom NGSS augmentation paper.
proposed_changes.rationale: The paper adds classroom/in-term evidence for synthetic response augmentation under held-out real-response evaluation and explicit validity-work caveats.

proposed_changes.summary: Stage extraction of New Mexico MSSA operational synthetic training augmentation workflow.
proposed_changes.rationale: The stakes claim needs structured fields distinguishing summative purpose, accountability consequence, training use, review/approval, and validation boundaries.

proposed_changes.summary: Stage extraction of high-stakes synthetic off-topic detection workflow.
proposed_changes.rationale: The claim needs a separate high-stakes stress-test/detection boundary so detection evidence is not overread as scoring validation.

proposed_changes.summary: Stage extraction of formative communication-skills synthetic training augmentation.
proposed_changes.rationale: The claim needs lower-stakes professional-education evidence with real-response evaluation and summative human-review caveats.

proposed_changes.summary: Stage extraction of classroom NGSS GPT augmentation workflow.
proposed_changes.rationale: The claim needs in-term/classroom evidence that separates training augmentation from grade-bearing or high-stakes decisions.

proposed_changes.summary: Stage finding that operational state summative use supports limited synthetic training augmentation.
proposed_changes.rationale: This is the strongest new direct evidence and changes the prior high-stakes evidence boundary.

proposed_changes.summary: Stage finding that high-stakes synthetic off-topic examples support detection rather than score validation.
proposed_changes.rationale: This keeps adjacent high-stakes evidence in the correct workflow role.

proposed_changes.summary: Stage finding on formative real-plus-synthetic training augmentation and summative human-review limits.
proposed_changes.rationale: This evidence informs lower-stakes acceptable use while preserving real-evaluation boundaries.

proposed_changes.summary: Stage finding on classroom synthetic augmentation and validity-work limits.
proposed_changes.rationale: This evidence informs in-term/classroom use without overclaiming grade-bearing or operational validation.

proposed_changes.summary: Stage targeted search and screening protocol for consequential assessment use boundaries.
proposed_changes.rationale: The update needs auditable search history and screening decisions for the new direct and adjacent evidence.

proposed_changes.summary: Update the stakes-boundary claim to include direct operational state summative training-augmentation evidence.
proposed_changes.rationale: The previous claim said direct high-stakes evidence was thin and mostly adjacent; the New Mexico technical report requires a narrower but more affirmative high-stakes training-augmentation category.

proposed_claim_implications.note: Changes the high-stakes boundary from mostly adjacent/diagnostic evidence to direct but tightly controlled operational training-augmentation evidence.
After review, consider a separate acceptable-use update because the New Mexico report may add operational training augmentation to the acceptable-use taxonomy.

next_actions: Publication complete. Review downstream pages if this change affects shared surfaces.
applicability_boundary: Keep the staged does_not_apply_when and limitations language that excludes synthetic-only validation, cut scores, subgroup fairness, and live scoring decisions.
construct_mapping: Keep Wang et al. categorized as adjacent high-stakes detection/stress-test evidence, as staged.
method_classification: Keep generation_model and prompt_strategy bounded to reported facts, as staged.
scorer_validation_relevance: Retain the staged claim language that says this strengthens controlled high-stakes training augmentation but not synthetic-only final validation.
scorer_validation_relevance: Keep the support role as boundary condition or adjacent boundary evidence.
source_fidelity: Keep the staged claim limited to training augmentation and retain the next action to check validation-set composition before making stronger validation language.
source_fidelity: Keep the artifact language that reports GPT-4 while explicitly flagging the apparent GPT-5 label for review.
synthesis_overreach: Keep the staged limitation that the claim does not generalize one New Mexico MSSA report into a broad institutional accountability or vendor policy.
